title: The burden of disease vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: The burden of disease is measured in Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs). DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life. Here it is age-standardized and expressed per 100,000 individuals.
caption:
The burden of disease vs. GDP per capita

The burden of disease is measured in Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs). DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life. Here it is age-standardized and expressed per 100,000 individuals.

This scatter plot displays the relationship between the burden of disease and GDP per capita for various countries in 2023. The vertical axis represents DALYs per 100,000 people, ranging from 0 to 80,000, while the horizontal axis shows GDP per capita in international dollars at 2021 prices on a logarithmic scale from $2,000 to $100,000. Circle sizes correspond to population, with a reference scale indicating sizes for 600 million and 1.4 billion people. Countries are color-coded by region: North America (orange), South America (red-brown), Africa (purple), Europe (blue), Asia (teal), and Oceania (light blue). A clear negative correlation is visible: countries with lower GDP per capita generally exhibit higher DALY rates. The Central African Republic sits at the top left with the highest DALY rate near 80,000 and a GDP per capita below $2,000. Other African nations like Niger, Chad, Burundi, and DR Congo cluster in the upper-left quadrant with DALYs between 60,000 and 70,000 and low GDP. In contrast, high-income countries such as the United States, Germany, Japan, and Singapore occupy the bottom right, with DALYs below 30,000 and GDP per capita exceeding $40,000. Large-population countries like India, China, and Indonesia appear as large teal circles in the middle of the chart, with DALYs ranging from approximately 20,000 to 40,000 and GDP per capita between $5,000 and $20,000. Greenland appears as an outlier with high GDP but relatively elevated DALYs compared to other high-income nations.
